refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to operator to maintain safe control.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams.
DP4: Use redundant communication paths to improve reliability.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression to reduce latency.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses the latency FR with minimal coupling to other system functions; prioritization and redundancy are handled elsewhere.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol overhead causing unexpected latency spikes.
Risk that compression may introduce processing delays.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication stack responsible for teleoperation command and telemetry processing.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between vehicle and teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation data.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from operator to teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle module processes commands but latency optimization is not contained here.
Safety services monitor but do not implement latency control.
Overall system level, but latency control is better localized.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link quality outside Teleoperation control.
Risk of latency-related logic spreading into Vehicle module if not carefully managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts and monitoring to prevent diffusion of latency logic into Vehicle or other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear performance focus and minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion of latency logic into Vehicle module if interfaces are not strictly enforced.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Latency control is well contained in Teleoperation module, supporting ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring tools at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and control interface boundaries to avoid diffusion of latency logic.
Plan for fallback or escalation to edge computing if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely feedback to operator to maintain safe control.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Latency measurement tools are accurate and available.
open_risks: Potential latency spikes due to network variability.
Diffusion of latency logic into other modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from telemetry under operational conditions.
Interface compliance audits between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
